context: Tests whether the agent resolves a non-trivial merge conflict by understanding the intent of both branches rather than picking a side. One branch added structured error handling with a PaymentResult type (retryable flag, errorCode), the other added multi-currency support with a ChargeReceipt type (settledAmountCents, currency). A correct resolution merges both concerns into a single return type and preserves the try/catch error handling while also surfacing settled amount and currency.

checklist.name: Reads and explains both sides before resolving
checklist.description: The agent explicitly describes what each branch was trying to accomplish — not just what the code says, but the intent (error handling for callers vs. settled-amount transparency for FX). This must appear before the resolution, not as a post-hoc summary.

checklist.name: Preserves error handling from HEAD branch
checklist.description: The merged result retains the try/catch block, the retryable flag, and the distinction between network failures and hard declines. It must not revert to a version that lets Stripe errors propagate uncaught.

checklist.name: Preserves settled-amount fields from incoming branch
checklist.description: The merged return type includes settledAmountCents and currency sourced from the Stripe response (charge.amount_captured, charge.currency), not just echoing back the input amountCents and currency.

checklist.name: No leftover conflict markers
checklist.description: The output file contains no git conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>). The file is valid TypeScript syntax.

checklist.name: Merged return type is coherent
checklist.description: The final return type (whether named PaymentResult, ChargeReceipt, or something new) includes all necessary fields from both branches: success/errorCode/retryable AND chargeId/settledAmountCents/currency. It does not duplicate or contradict itself.

checklist.name: Does not invent new behavior
checklist.description: The resolution only merges what both branches intended — it does not add new fields, change error codes, alter the function signature beyond what was necessary to unify the two return types, or introduce retry logic.
